location.name: Irvine; Los Angeles; New York; San Francisco; San Jose

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
WHAT YOU’LL DO  

Own and drive revenue growth across a Tier 1 account portfolio in Thailand and Vietnam, with full accountability for commercial outcomes
Articulate the strategic value of The Trade Desk platform to senior client and agency stakeholders. Translating technical capability into business outcomes, not just product demonstrations.
Understand competitive differentiators in fast-changing online advertising landscape
Act as a strategic advisor to senior marketing and agency stakeholders -  challenging client thinking, shaping long-term investment strategy, and holding yourself accountable to their business outcomes, not just their campaign KPIs.
Develop long-term account roadmaps and joint business plans with Tier 1 clients, anchoring the relationship to multi-year commercial outcomes rather than campaign-by-campaign activity. Partner with the Trading team to accelerate campaign performance and unlock new revenue opportunities within existing accounts
Collaborate with the Business Development team to identify and convert growth opportunities across the TH and VN portfolio.
Build and maintain senior-level relationships across client and agency organizations. Engaging decision-makers at CMO, regional, and global level.
Work with Product Management to help define features and drive enhancements to The Trade Desk platform and any other auxiliary element where synergies can be created (insights, measurement, billing, marketing, innovation)
Help build the Client Services practice, by creating training materials, best practices, sales materials and customer facing guides 

 WHO YOU ARE:  

 You are a commercially ambitious client leader who builds senior relationships and uses them to drive strategic change, not just to maintain them. You challenge clients to think differently about how they invest, you form and hold strong points of view on what works, and you hold yourself accountable to their business outcomes rather than just campaign delivery. You are as comfortable presenting a multi-year account strategy to a regional CMO as you are rolling up your sleeves to troubleshoot a campaign with a trading team. You bring existing credibility in the Thailand and/or Vietnam market, and you are energized by the opportunity to build something, not just manage what's already there.  

What you bring to the table: 

9+ years of client facing experience in digital advertising, with a clear track record of owning and exceeding revenue goals
Demonstrated ability to grow Tier 1 accounts through consultative, long-term client relationships   with clients -  agencies and direct advertisers
A challenger mindset: you push back on client briefs when you see a better way, you form strong points of view on investment strategy, and you are comfortable having difficult commercial conversations at senior levels
Effective time management skills - ability to prioritize and meet deadlines
Experience with driving projects requiring stakeholders from different disciplines to drive output at pace
Ability to translate technical capability into strategic recommendations 
Deep understanding of the programmatic advertising ecosystem, DSPs, RTB, data strategy, measurement, and competitive landscape 
Strong analytical and data storytelling skills: you turn performance data and market intelligence into compelling narratives that drive client decisions, not just reporting decks
Experience navigating the complexity - Outstanding troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving abilities, along with an ability to collaborate cross-functionally in a fast-paced environment 

The Trade Desk does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired; such resumes will be deemed the sole property of The Trade Desk. The Trade Desk is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment will be based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

Bellevue; Chicago; New York; San Francisco; San Jose

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
What we do
The Trade Desk is transforming the way global brands and agencies reach audiences through
the open internet. In Korea—a strategically critical and fast-growing market in APAC—our
Business Development team plays a central role in driving this transformation. As a Business
Development Director in Korea, you will help to lead our commercial efforts across key
domestic and outbound advertising brands as well as agency holding companies.
You’ll drive meaningful partnerships, elevate client relationships to a strategic level, and unlock
scalable, long-term revenue opportunities for the business. This is a senior-level role requiring a
proven track record in building trusted relationships, delivering commercial results, and
fostering strong cross-functional collaboration across internal and external stakeholders.
We are proud of the culture we’ve built at The Trade Desk. We believe in curiosity, humility,
and collaboration, and we value the unique experiences and perspectives that each person
brings. We are committed to creating an inclusive environment where everyone can do their
best work and be their authentic selves.
If you’re commercially driven, creative, and excited to be part of a dynamic, globally connected
team that’s reshaping the future of digital advertising in Korea and beyond—we want to talk.

What you’ll do
accounts.
• Leads business development efforts for The Trade Desk’s largest or most strategic
• Proactively builds and maintains relationships with top executives at brand advertisers
(upstream stakeholders).
• Acts as a master relationship builder, sales leader, and creative problem solver with
strong emotional intelligence and professionalism.
• Serves as a consultative partner to clients and internal stakeholders alike.
• Accurately manage and communicate monthly forecasts and pipeline.
• Partners closely with GMs and VPs to define and execute strategic initiatives.
• Leads collaboration with cross-functional teams to ensure effective client management.
• Thinks beyond core sales responsibilities to understand and contribute to the business
holistically.
• Represents The Trade Desk at regional industry events and serves as a public-facing
voice for the company.
• Understands and appropriately applies DOA (Delegation of Authority) in decision-
making.
• Identifies opportunities to improve operational efficiency and generate revenue;
sponsors execution of key strategic projects.
• Acts as a bridge between Business Development and senior TTD leadership, ensuring
alignment and communication.
• Participates in product feedback sessions and working groups when invited, sharing
client and market insights.
• Takes personal ownership of company success and drives impact at a market level.
• Evangelizes TTD’s values and vision to industry leaders; regularly presents at industry
events to shape market perception.
• Contribute to The Trade Desk culture by bringing your full self.
Who you are
• Proven track record in business development and local/outbound sales within the
Korean advertising ecosystem, with deep knowledge of the local media landscape,
agency structures, and cultural nuances.
• Leads strategic partnerships with top-tier Korean advertisers and agency holding groups.
• Drives new business growth by identifying, pitching, and converting high-potential
accounts across verticals.
• Builds and nurtures long-term, consultative relationships with key decision-makers at
both brands and agencies.
• Holds existing senior-level relationships with major advertisers and agencies in Korea.
• Serves as a senior leader within the Korea BD team, mentoring and enabling junior and
mid-level sellers to think and operate strategically.
• Demonstrated success in sales leadership roles, consistently exceeding revenue targets
and driving sustainable growth.
• Skilled in building from the ground up—an agile, proactive self-starter who thrives in
fast-changing and ambiguous environments.
• Brings strong negotiation experience, including leading complex commercial deals with
regional and global accounts.
• Strong quantitative and analytical skills, with the ability to translate data into actionable
commercial strategies.
• Comfortable engaging in technical product discussions, including roadmap prioritization
and platform scoping.
• Effective communicator with strong executive presence, able to confidently engage and
influence C-level stakeholders.
• Passionate about closing deals, owning the room, and driving measurable results.
• Collaborates effectively with cross-functional teams, including product, marketing, client
services, finance, and legal.
• Able to advocate for Korean market needs within global teams while aligning with
broader company strategy.
• Active contributor to global initiatives, including multi-market client growth, product
feedback loops, and international expansion.
• Bilingual fluency in Korean and English, enabling effective communication across global
and local teams.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Job Title: Director, Business Development
Location:  1114 6th Avenue, New York, NY 10110
*Telecommuting permitted: work may be performed within normal commuting distance from The Trade Desk, Inc. office in New York, NY
 
Job Duties: Be the primary contact for customers and be in regular communication with customers to assess customer satisfaction and react to any issues. Compile industry trend data and campaign and spend data from various platforms to assemble client facing materials for the purpose of selling through products. Collect and analyze data related to customers target demographics and users, advising them on best practices of reaching intended audience. Use various platforms to analyze spend and marketing activity data to present back to customers to illustrate successful use of product. Help companies form their marketing strategy as it relates to digital and programmatic channels. Forecast all potential activity for customers and analyze various trends in marketing spend for those partners helping them to optimize activity. Responsible for being expertly aware of customers competitors, helping them to create unique marketing strategies. Closely monitor industry trends and trade literature to expertly speak to customers about state of the industry and guide them to adapt strategies. Attend tradeconferences to meet with customers and prospective customers educating them on company software. Develop and implement various procedures for company teams to service customers.
 
Salary: $117,042 - $179,400/year
 
Job Requirements: Bachelor’s degree (U.S. or foreign equivalent) in Marketing, Marketing Management or related field and eight (8) years of experience in the job offered or related role. Must have three (3) years of sales experience working for an online publisher, ad network, ad exchange, ad server, or senior agency. Must have three (3) years of experience meetingcompany revenue goals, including developing strong client relationships at media agencies, pitching capabilities and customizing ad tech products and services to meet their programmaticneeds, and using Salesforce to track and report revenue. Must have three (3) years of communication experience, including speaking with C-level clients to pitch ad tech products and services within a large media agency. Must have three (3) years of negotiation experience including building commercial endeavors and rate cards for a media agency client. Must haveone (1) year of experience creating relationships with decision-makers at enterprise client level,including having directly sold media or ad tech services to programmatic teams and Strategic Investments teams. Domestic travel required up to 10% of the time to visit client sites.

#LI-DNI

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Business Development Director, North Asia Outbound
What we do
We are looking for an ambitious, high-performing, and hunter-minded business development leader to join our fast-growing North Asia Outbound team. In this role, you will focus on driving regional market expansion by hunting new business opportunities and delivering exceptional growth for our clients.
Your core mission is to actively prospect and win top cross-border brands expanding outbound from North Asia. You will act as a trusted solution seller for major exporters and rapid-growth emerging verticals, such as Social Apps and AI. This i role requires strong sales acumen, a relentless drive to exceed revenue targets, and the ability to foster deep, value-add relationships with C-level executives.

What you’ll do
●	Hunt New Outbound Clients: Proactively identify, prospect, and close new business opportunities across North Asia (Greater China, Korea and Japan).
●	Drive Client Growth & Revenue: Deliver significant sales results and exceed revenue targets through strategic selling, pipeline management, and relentless execution.
●	Execute Solution Selling: Deeply understand your clients' macro business goals, challenges, and KPIs to design true value-add digital advertising solutions that drive measurable business results.
●	Build a Powerful Network: Establish and maintain a dominant presence within the North Asia export ecosystem, building deep, trusted relationships with C-level clients and key industry partners.
●	Champion Emerging Verticals: Target and scale high-potential emerging outbound verticals, with a specific focus on cross-border Social applications and AI-driven platforms.
●	Advocate for Client Success: Collaborate closely with global product and engineering teams to communicate market needs and identify product gaps, ensuring our platform features match your clients' global expansion requirements.
Who you are
●	Proven Sales Hunter: 10+ years of online advertising experience, with a heavy and successful background in outbound sales, lead generation, and new business development.
●	Strategic Solution Seller: Exceptional ability to engage, influence, and build trusted relationships with C-level executives, moving beyond transactional selling to offer true value-add solutions.
●	North Asia Ecosystem Expert: Deep knowledge of the digital advertising landscape supporting brands expanding globally, with a proven track record and existing network within the GRCN market.
●	Emerging Vertical Savvy: Familiarity with or strong exposure to cross-border advertising, publisher networks, ad tech, or DSP environments, particularly serving fast-growing sectors like Social apps and AI.
●	Results-Driven Competitor: Possess a high degree of sales acumen, exceptional time management, and a results-oriented mindset that strives to consistently excel and deliver on revenue goals.
●	Adaptable & Resilient: Proven ability to navigate complex, fast-paced, and ambiguous environments while maintaining a strong, positive focus on executing sales targets.
●	Multilingual Communicator: Fluency in both English and Mandarin is required to successfully manage the GRCN portfolio. Proficiency in a third language (such as Korean, Japanese, or Cantonese) is a significant plus.

jobs.title: Business Development Director, North Asia Outbound

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Job Title: Data Scientist II
Location: 400 Spectrum Center Drive, Suite 550, Irvine, CA 92618

*Telecommuting permitted: work may be performed within normal commuting distance from The Trade Desk, Inc. office in Irvine, CA.

Job Duties: Design, implement, and optimize scalable graph-based algorithms to enhance internal graph products. Optimize large-scale algorithmic systems operating on terabyte-scale datasets using distributed computing frameworks. Develop custom data science solutions from first principles, leveraging advanced techniques in probability and statistics, machine learning, and graph mining, especially in contexts where off-the-shelf models are ineffective. Analyze large datasets and build statistical models at scale on top of terabytes worth of data to generate actionable insights for products improvement. Conduct scientific analysis to validate third-party data and evaluate whether they are useful for products. Conduct best-subset selection using information theory-based approaches to enhance targeting and performance strategies. Automate and monitor workflows using orchestration tools (e.g. Apache Airflow) to ensure reliable data pipeline execution. Own the end-to-end data products lifecycle, including identifying research questions, exploratory data analysis, methods design, development, testing, deployment, maintenance, and presenting results to relevant stakeholders. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure technical design, implementation, and delivery of data science projects align with business objectives and product roadmap.

Salary: $130,270 - $170,600 / year

Job Requirements: Bachelor's degree (U.S. or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science, Data Science, or related field and four (4) years of experience in the job offered or related role. Must have four (4) years of experience with: one or more programming languages; SQL; cloud computing platforms; version control systems and CI/CD pipelines; data analysis and visualization for large-scale datasets; building and evaluating machine learning models; applying probability and statistical methods to build and evaluate data-driven products; and Agile. Must have two (2) years of experience with: developing scalable graph algorithms; large-scale graph mining; distributed computing frameworks including Spark; and workflow orchestration tools.

jobs.title: General Manager, Revenue Operations (Agency and Growth)
GM, Global Sales Learning and Effectiveness
GTM Strategy and Operations Director - EMEA

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
The Trade Desk is changing the way global brands and their agencies advertise to audiences around the world. How? With a media buying platform that helps brands deliver a more insightful and relevant ad experience for consumers –– and sets a new standard for global reach, accuracy, and transparency. We are proud of the culture we have built. We value the unique experiences and perspectives that each person brings to The Trade Desk, and we are committed to fostering inclusive spaces where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work every day.

So, if you are talented, driven, creative, and eager to join a dynamic, globally-connected team, then we want to talk!

What you'll do:
Oversee the health of agency holding group and/or independent agency relationships as a strategic advisor, guiding problem-solving discussions and providing best-in-class client services.
Demonstrate the value of The Trade Desk to clients by providing innovative solutions that meet the clients’ needs and our business goals.
Lead key agency client meetings in collaboration with business development and trading counterparts.
Develop trusting and productive relationships with key stakeholders both internally and externally.
Lead the development of portfolio-specific goals based on the company’s vision and values and responsible for tracking progress of portfolio revenue goals on a monthly and quarterly basis.
Become heavily involved in product development by identifying how to build product that provides solutions for our clients’ business objectives and serving as a liaison between our clients and our product team.
Coach a small and nimble team of Account Managers; overseeing their day-to-day management, motivating the team by setting clear expectations, developing career path plans, providing constructive feedback, and maintaining a high level of performance from the team to develop the next generation of leaders.
Manage for team bandwidth, delivering a high-level of account support and reporting progress against goals to management.
Communicate effectively with other functional leaders by solving business challenges together, identifying opportunities for growth, and collaborating to drive efficiency and effectiveness across the organization.
Who you are:

You are an effective client-facing leader who is skilled in building and nurturing client relationships while also leading an agile team and developing talent. We are looking for an individual possessing excellent communication, strategic and leadership skills to serve as a player-coach across a portfolio of accounts.
7+ years of digital advertising experience with at least 2 directly managing and developing a team
Experience in developing, nurturing and managing client relationships.
Proven track record of driving revenue growth with large customer accounts.
Strong management and leadership skills through a collaborative team approach.
Ability to build strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, executive team and industry groups.
Execute (and coach others to execute) timely in fast-paced environment through effective time management and prioritization skills.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
Ambition to develop personally and nurture the development of others.
Excellent influencing and negotiation skills.
Highly organized with a strong attention to detail.

The Trade Desk does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired; such resumes will be deemed the sole property of The Trade Desk. The Trade Desk is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment will be based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
The Trade Desk is changing the way global brands and their agencies advertise to audiences around the world. How? With a media buying platform that helps brands deliver a more insightful and relevant ad experience for consumers –– and sets a new standard for global reach, accuracy, and transparency. We are proud of the culture we have built. We value the unique experiences and perspectives that each person brings to The Trade Desk, and we are committed to fostering inclusive spaces where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work every day.

So, if you are talented, driven, creative, and eager to join a dynamic, globally-connected team, then we want to talk!

What you'll do:

Manage and grow a roster of key accounts by building strong client relationships, providing strategic recommendations and exceptional client service.
Collaborate with the Trading team to rapidly onboard new customers and provide data driven insights to clients.
Collaborate with the Business Development team to identify ways to grow current accounts.
Become an expert on the use and best practices on The Trade Desk platform and offer insights to clients on how to improve campaigns and meet KPIs.
Provide continual training to clients as new features are added to The Trade Desk.
Work with Product Management to help define features and drive enhancements to The Trade Desk platform.
Help build the Client Services practice, by creating training materials, best practices, sales materials and customer facing guides.

Who you are: 

2 + years of consultative, client-facing work with online advertising experience preferred
Outstanding troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving abilities, along with an ability to collaborate cross-functionally in a fast-paced start-up environment.
Proven track record of growing accounts by forging a strong relationship with clients, understanding their objectives and recommending best strategies.
Effective time management skills – ability to prioritize and meet deadlines.
Ability to collaborate across multiple teams and internal stakeholders as well as work independently on daily tasks.
Willingness to travel alongside a team up to 25% of the time.
Someone who is looking to grow into more senior level opportunities in the region.

The Trade Desk does not accept unsolicited resumes from search firm recruiters. Fees will not be paid in the event a candidate submitted by a recruiter without an agreement in place is hired; such resumes will be deemed the sole property of The Trade Desk. The Trade Desk is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment will be based on merit, competence, performance, and business needs. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, marital status, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic information, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other status protected under federal, state, or local law.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Job Title: Director, Business Development
Location:  1114 6th Avenue, New York, NY 10110
*Telecommuting permitted: work may be performed within normal commuting distance from The Trade Desk, Inc. office in New York, NY
 
Job Duties: Be the primary contact for customers and be in regular communication with customers to assess customer satisfaction and react to any issues. Compile industry trend data and campaign and spend data from various platforms to assemble client facing materials for the purpose of selling through products. Collect and analyze data related to customers target demographics and users, advising them on best practices of reaching intended audience. Use various platforms to analyze spend and marketing activity data to present back to customers to illustrate successful use of product. Help companies form their marketing strategy as it relates to digital and programmatic channels. Forecast all potential activity for customers and analyze various trends in marketing spend for those partners helping them to optimize activity. Responsible for being expertly aware of customers competitors, helping them to create unique marketing strategies. Closely monitor industry trends and trade literature to expertly speak to customers about state of the industry and guide them to adapt strategies. Attend tradeconferences to meet with customers and prospective customers educating them on company software. Develop and implement various procedures for company teams to service customers.
 
Salary: $117,042 - $179,400/year
 
Job Requirements: Bachelor’s degree (U.S. or foreign equivalent) in Marketing, Marketing Management or related field and eight (8) years of experience in the job offered or related role. Must have three (3) years of sales experience working for an online publisher, ad network, ad exchange, ad server, or senior agency. Must have three (3) years of experience meetingcompany revenue goals, including developing strong client relationships at media agencies, pitching capabilities and customizing ad tech products and services to meet their programmaticneeds, and using Salesforce to track and report revenue. Must have three (3) years of communication experience, including speaking with C-level clients to pitch ad tech products and services within a large media agency. Must have three (3) years of negotiation experience including building commercial endeavors and rate cards for a media agency client. Must haveone (1) year of experience creating relationships with decision-makers at enterprise client level,including having directly sold media or ad tech services to programmatic teams and Strategic Investments teams. Domestic travel required up to 10% of the time to visit client sites.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
What we do: The Trading Team works with Account Management and Business Development to provide strategic recommendations to retain clients and grow their business.  The main responsibilities of a Trading Specialist are to provide detailed performance and pacing recommendations and be a platform expert who can not only teach the nuances to clients but can actively navigate and utilize the platform themselves.  Trading Specialists should eventually be viewed as a consultant to clients who are aiming to drive the strongest performance possible. 

What you’ll do: 

Spearhead strategy development to ensure clients stay competitive in key areas including first-party data management and activation, CTV consolidation and retail measurement. 

Lead data-driven conversations utilizing TTD platform and custom reporting to provide insights and tell client relevant stories to provide a full picture of advertising’s impact on business results. 

Consult with clients on their campaign goals and strategies to ensure they are maximizing the value TTD’s platform provides 

Operate as a power user of TTD’s UI and empower clients to also become power users to most effectively plan, execute, optimize and build on successful advertising campaigns. 

Collaborate with Account Management and Sales Executives to educate your clients, grow your portfolio of business and ensure buyers are up-to-date on TTD’s constantly evolving suite of products and solutions 

Build and own key relationships with client planning, trading and analytical teams. 

Collaborate with engineers, data scientists and product managers to solve complex technical problems including cross-platform frequency analysis, consumer journey visualizations and API solutions. 

Troubleshoot advanced technical problems including performance issues, measurement discrepancies and constrained targeting. 

 

Who you are: 

3+ years of consultative, client-facing work in ad tech (DSP experience is a plus!), digital marketing, finance or other data-heavy industries. 

Eager to constantly learn and adapt in a fast-paced environment that requires self-driven education and improvement. 

Outstanding problem-solving and analytical skills with the ability to distill complex, data-heavy information into simple but powerful stories and insights. 

Strong interest in learning the underlying mechanics of a platform and the ability to act as a conduit between clients and product teams to consistently improve TTD’s platform over time. 

Comfortable with cross-functional collaboration with the ability to navigate ambiguous and complex problems and situations. 

Effective time management skills, especially the ability to prioritize and meet deadlines. 

Advanced MS Excel skills and the ability to efficiently wrangle extremely large data sets (R, Python, PowerBI and other analytical language and tool experience is a plus!).

jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - Price Optimization & Bidding

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value: About the Role:Our Software Engineers are end-to-end owners. You will participate actively in all aspects of designing, building, and delivering products for our clients.We have dozens of individual, mission-focused teams working across a wide spectrum of technological challenges. You will have the opportunity, depending on your interests and aptitude, to work on large-scale distributed systems coordinating thousands of servers in cloud and physical data centers around the world, petabyte-scale data challenges, machine learning, advanced visualizations, and interactive user interfaces – to name a few.Software Engineers contribute to more than our product – they build up our team. Through a combination of mentoring, technical leadership, and/or direct management of small teams, they make others better and raise the bar for those around them. Who We Are Looking For:You have a sustained track record of making significant, self-directed, and end-to-end contributions to large and impactful software projects. You think beyond just the task at hand to deeply understand the 'why' behind what you are doing. You understand engineering fundamentals. At our scale, many off-the-shelf techniques and existing technologies (open source and enterprise) simply don't work. You are able to work from first principles to evaluate solutions and adapt them to a unique environment. You are a broadly skilled engineer, accustomed to developing web services. You have experience building always-on systems, working across a variety of technologies and service layers.You have a product-focused mindset.  You have the passion and ability to contribute to the process of discovering what will delight our clients and push forward one of the world’s largest and most influential industries toward a vision of openness, transparency, and evidence-based decision-making.You work with confidence and without ego. Our engineers have deep knowledge and exercise a high degree of leadership in their daily work. You have strongly-held, defensible ideas, and advocate for what you believe is right. You are also adept at identifying and evaluating trade-offs, willing to be proven wrong, and quick to walk through fire to support your fellow teammates. You value, seek out, and foster diversity. We are a global team from many diverse backgrounds, with different experiences and perspectives. To complement this team, you will welcome ideas that are different from your own and be skilled at finding and building from common ground. You are a creative thinker, not bound by "the way things have always been done". What you know is less important than how well you learn and innovate. We don't need engineers who know all the answers; we need engineers who can invent the answers no one has thought of yet, to the questions yet to be asked.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Job Title: Software Engineer I 
Location: 400 Spectrum Center Drive, Suite 550, Irvine, CA 92618

*Telecommuting permitted: work may be performed within normal commuting distance from The Trade Desk, Inc. office in Irvine, CA.

Job Duties: Implement full stack software solutions utilizing relational and high-performance databases, back-end application development and API design, modern JavaScript frameworks and component-based UI architectures, and deployment and operation in cloud-based, containerized environments. Act as a company-wide subject matter expert on measurement solutions including, but not limited to, The Trade Desk Conversion Lift, Third Party Conversion Lift, Carrier Activation Indicator, New Devices Indicator, Inferred Brand Intent, and Campaign Reporting and Attribution. Proactively monitor deployed solutions for bugs, performance concerns, and feedback using tools like NewRelic, Sumologic, and Grafana to ensure maximum operational excellence and user satisfaction. Include automated unit, integration, and end-to-end tests in the delivery of all solutions in order to prevent regressions and accelerate team’s development velocity. Design complex database tables, GraphQL schemas, and client access patterns to satisfy product requirements and address scalability concerns. Identify and lead implementation of new measurement solutions that can be built on top of The Trade Desk’s infrastructure and substantial data. Collaborate with product managers and designers using tools like Figma to influence project directions and general user experiences. Track project progress using tools like Jira in order to facilitate roadmap discussions with product managers.

Salary: $137,600 - $206,400 / year

Job Requirements: Bachelor's degree (U.S. or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science or related field. Position requires working knowledge of the following skills gained through coursework or experience: SQL database; object-oriented programming; API design; Javascript and UI frameworks; and cloud infrastructure.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Job Title: Software Engineer II
Location:  425 Market Street, Suite 2400, San Francisco, CA 94105

*Telecommuting permitted: work may be performed within normal commuting distance from The Trade Desk, Inc. office in San Francisco, CA.
Job Duties: Design, develop, and maintain backend services and system components using object oriented languages such as Java, Scala, or Python, following internal coding standards and design guidelines. Design and implement data processing workflows using Apache Spark or other large-scale distributed data processing frameworks to support business analytics and product requirements. Develop high-performance and scalable data access layers, including implementing algorithms for efficient querying, caching strategies, and low-latency data retrieval. Implement and maintain cloud-native services deployed on container orchestration platforms (e.g. Kubernetes, ECS) and integrate with cloud infrastructure components (e.g. IAM policies, load balancers, service meshes). Design and validate API contracts, service interfaces, and cross-service communication patterns, including REST, RPC, or asynchronous messaging. Create and maintain CI/CD pipelines (e.g. Gitlab CI), including automated build processes, test execution, and controlled deployment workflows to ensure reliable software delivery. Instrument applications for observability by implementing metrics, structured logs, or distributed tracing to improve system transparency and operational insight. Investigate and resolve
software defects or performance issues by analyzing logs, system metrics, and profiling results, collaborating with relevant teams as needed. Collaborate with product and engineering teams to convert business and functional requirements into technical specifications and system designs with clear performance and reliability goals.

Salary: $151,400 - $227,000/year

Job Requirements: Bachelor's degree (U.S. or foreign equivalent) in Computer Science, Computer Engineering or related field. Position requires working knowledge of the following skills gained through coursework or experience: at least one programming language, including Java, Scala, or Python; container orchestration platforms, including Kubernetes, and containerization technologies, including Docker; designing and validating API contracts and service interfaces, including REST, RPC, or asynchronous messaging patterns; implementing CI/CD workflows, including Gitlab CI, and automate builds, testing and controlled deployments; observability practices, including metrics, structured logging, or distributed tracing instrumentation; and diagnosing and debugging software issues using logs, system metrics, or profiling tools.

location.name: Chicago; Irvine; Los Angeles; New York; San Francisco; San Jose; Seattle; Ventura

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
Who we are

The Trade Desk is changing the way global brands and their agencies advertise to audiences around the world. How? With a media-buying platform that helps brands deliver a more insightful and relevant ad experience for consumers — and sets a new standard for global reach, accuracy, and transparency. We are proud of the culture we have built. We value the unique experiences and perspectives that each person brings to The Trade Desk, and we are committed to fostering inclusive spaces where everyone can bring their authentic selves to work every day.

So if you’re talented, driven, creative, and hungry to join a dynamic, globally-connected team, then we want to talk!

The Trade Desk is seeking a China country specialist at the Senior Accountant level based in our Shanghai office. Reporting to the Assistant Controller, a successful candidate will have a good understanding of financial accounting and China compliance and tax requirements, be hands-on in resolving accounting operation issues and able to provide solutions drawing on past experiences. The Senior Accountant will work as an individual contributor and collaborate with other stakeholders (e.g., stakeholders within the organization, external third-party consultants, external auditors, etc.)


What you’ll do 
•	Perform month end close tasks including but not limited to, preparation of journal entries for cash activities, accruals, reserves, intercompany transactions and other business transactions, account reconciliations and reporting under US GAAP and China GAAP for China legal entities.
•	Collect, compile, verify and analyze information required for the preparation of statutory and compliance reports, including preparation or review of financial statements, analytical reports and regulatory or tax filings (i.e. VAT and CIT declarations).
•	Manage third party external service providers that provide statutory compliance, tax consultancy, treasury, and statutory audit. 
•	Act as the first point of contact for local tax authorities in China and respond to their requests in timely manner.
•	Collaborate with accounting operations functions, including credit and collections, accounts payable, payroll and stock admin teams, to provide country-level support.
•	Act as a finance business partner for business, engineering and support teams in China, assisting internal stakeholders while escalating concerns to appropriate levels of the controllership organization.
•	Develop initiatives or takes on duties in process improvement and reporting enhancement projects with the goal of promoting efficiency, effective customer service and/or improved employee relations.
•	Builds strong working relationships with internal and external stakeholders, with the ability to steer through organizational mazes to get things done.
•	Understand organizational processes and make recommendations when adopting global standards to local practices. 
•	Operate as a country financial liaison providing early alerts on accounting and finance concerns to appropriate levels of the organization.
•	Support new initiatives and special projects, as required.

Who you are
•	7+ years of progressive experience in accounting, tax and compliance at a US technology company.
•	Knowledge in China GAAP, application of local tax and compliance regulations is a must. Previous Big 4 audit experience is a plus.
•	Bachelor's degree in finance/accounting, CICPA preferred.
•	Skilled Oracle or ERP system user, with, or interested in, working with more technical/data driven tools.
•	Detail-oriented self-starter with a strong sense of ownership, ability to work independently and see tasks/projects through to completion, meet deadlines and escalate matters, with little to no supervision.
•	Able to make effective and independent judgment, take appropriate courses of action, develop creative solutions to difficult problems and manage diverse and conflicting priorities.
•	Highly collaborative and enjoy partnering with others to seek out opportunities to improve process and efficiencies across the organization. 
•	Analytically driven with the ability to present information persuasively and effectively through writing, presentations and spreadsheets.
•	Excellent command of English, both oral and written, and comfortable working in an English-speaking environment.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
WHO WE ARE LOOKING FOR:
We are seeking a highly strategic, commercially minded, and technically savvy Senior Account Manager to oversee a premier portfolio of our most valuable brand-direct accounts. This role is designed for a senior client services professional who thrives on execution excellence, client strategy, and complex problem-solving. 

WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING:
1. Senior Client Engagement & Executional Excellence
•	Executive-Level Advisory: Serve as a trusted strategic consultant to senior client stakeholders and their partner ecosystems, translating complex market insights into winning, high-performing media strategies.
•	Conflict & Relationship Management: Confidently navigate ambiguous situations, manage complex external account dynamics, and resolve senior-level conflicts with maturity, poise, and strong negotiation skills.
•	Flawless Campaign Execution: Execute tasks timely and flawlessly in a fast-paced environment through masterful time management and sharp prioritization skills.
•	Platform Expertise: Maintain a rigorous, hands-on understanding of platform capabilities, applying operational best practices to ensure high-quality execution and campaign optimization.
2. Commercial & Portfolio Growth
•	Drive Revenue Retention & Expansion: Maintain primary ownership of a high-growth roster of key accounts, systematically identifying strategic cross-selling and upselling opportunities based on core business needs.
•	Business Development Alignment: Partner closely with the Business Development functions to successfully onboard major new customers and expand existing lines of business.

WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TABLE:
•	Bachelor’s Degree or above.
•	At least 5 years of online advertising experience required, 3+ years of client-facing experience, with proven track record of growing accounts by forging a strong relationship with clients.
•	Outstanding troubleshooting, analytical, and problem-solving abilities, along with an ability to collaborate cross-functionally in a fast-paced start-up environment.
•	Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills, with ability to influence decision-makers.
•	Hands-on expertise in programmatic advertising (DSP, SSP, DMP, MMP, Martech, etc.) or experience in brand/performance marketing in an agency or DTC company is a plus.
•	Proficiency in Excel and MS Office, data-driven mindset with the ability to translate insights into actionable recommendations.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
What you’ll do
•	Lead the development of a mix of brand direct and media agency portfolio as a senior strategic advisor, providing technical and client service excellence based on TTD's vision and values. 
•	Demonstrate the value of The Trade Desk by crafting innovative technical solutions that meet the clients’ needs and business goals while translating technical capabilities into practical business outcomes. 
•	Provide thought leadership to your client and their partner ecosystem on how to best leverage The Trade Desk’s platform and integration capabilities. 
•	Work closely with Business Development team to cultivate your accounts and become an extension of your client’s organization to solve their unique challenges through technology and automation. 
•	Responsible for growing your book of business through strategic cross selling and upselling opportunities by understanding the core client business needs. 
•	Own and drive the long-term success, growth, and operational excellence of assigned accounts, proactively identifying opportunities and solving business challenges across internal and external stakeholders. 
•	Act as the primary strategic owner of your portfolio, demonstrating strong commercial accountability across client retention, growth, forecasting, prioritisation, and client success outcomes. 
•	Develop trusting and productive relationships with key stakeholders both internally and externally. 
•	Operate effectively in a fast-paced and evolving environment, balancing multiple priorities while maintaining strong client leadership and execution excellence. 
•	Participate in product road map build out discussions based on client needs with international senior leadership, product & engineering teams. 
•	Communicate effectively with other functional leaders (product, marketing, inventory & partnership team) by solving business challenges together, identifying opportunities for growth, and collaborating to drive efficiency and effectiveness across the organisation. 
•	Maintain strong cross-pod relationships to support horizontal centre of excellence across account management and traders. 
 
Who you are
•	An experienced client leader who has a solid technical background and is skilled in building and nurturing client relationships. 
•	A proficient Mandarin and English speaker. 
•	We are looking for an individual possessing excellent communication, strategic thinking and leadership skills to serve as a player-coach across a portfolio of key accounts. Your duties will involve both managing relationships with clients and leading junior teammates. 
•	Extensive experience within the digital and programmatic industry in a client focused role. 
•	Experience establishing longstanding, consultative client relationships. 
•	Demonstrated ability to independently manage and grow complex client portfolios with a strong sense of ownership and accountability. 
•	Ability to build strong relationships with internal and external stakeholders, executive team and industry groups. 
•	Experience at an online publisher, agency, Mar Tech, SSP, DSP, or other online advertising role. 
•	Strong management and leadership skills through a collaborative team approach. 
•	A sense of maturity and ability to see the larger picture when making internal and external decisions and coaching your team to do the same. 
•	Repeatedly and consistently demonstrates confidence and good judgment in ambiguous situations, is experienced in diffusing difficult situations, and has a proven track record of resolving conflict. 
•	Execute, and coach others to execute, timely in fast-paced environment through effective time management and prioritisation skills. 
•	Comfortable engaging with senior client stakeholders and presenting strategic recommendations with confidence and credibility. 
•	Ambition to develop personally and nurture the development of others. 
•	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. 
•	Excellent influencing and negotiation skills. 
•	Highly organized with a strong attention to detail. 
•	Exemplars of TTD culture and values, teaching others how to act with grit, humility, generosity and poise. 
•	We are looking for someone who takes true ownership of client success — proactively driving growth, solving problems, and leading cross-functional collaboration without waiting for direction.

metadata.name: Job Description
metadata.value:
What we do 

The Trade Desk's Business Development team specialises on finding, building, and growing strategic partnerships with the most innovative China outbound advertisers and agencies.

Do you thrive on finding new ways to tackle a customer challenge? Are you a good listener who values empathy and integrity? Is your middle name ‘Grit’ and do you love collaboration, too? Do you enjoy developing long lasting, mutually beneficial relationships? Here at The Trade Desk, we're in search of a Senior Business Development Manager who is master relationship builder, strategic seller and creative problem solversto join our growing team.

What you’ll do 

• Acquire, build, and cultivate relationships with China outbound brands.
• Work collectively with Client Services team to cultivate and grow accounts.
• Communicate the value of The Trade Desk media buying platform by presenting our core value proposition, and tailor the content to suit the needs of each audience.
• Identify and close strategic up-selling opportunities by understanding core client business needs.
• Using knowledge of the market and competitors, identify and develop the company’s unique selling propositions and differentiators.
• Accurately manage and communicate monthly forecasts and pipeline.
• Contribute to The Trade Desk culture by bringing your full self.

Who you are 
• 8+ years of experience in digital advertising in outbound sales.
• Experience with gaming/apps vertical preferred.
• Programmatic platform sales experience preferred.
• Strong communication skills with the ability to speak with C-level clients.
• Existing relationships with decision-makers at China gaming/apps outbound brands a big plus.
• Strong quantitative and negotiation skills.
• Proven track record of exceeding revenue expectations.
• A passion and curiosity to learn.
• A consistent strive for excellence.

Job Duties: Design, build, deliver, and own full lifecycle development software from inception to production. Drive complex changes involving multiple components. Deliver quality changes, including automated tests, metrics, and monitoring. Guide and review changes to product features in line with product roadmaps. Build execution plans incorporating user data, observations, and market insight.
Work in a distributed system environment. Participate in and lead projects with a high level of technical ambiguity, adding significant new capabilities and establishing new patterns. Take on ownership and expertise in one or more components / functional areas of the system, and provide out for technical guidance.

Must have five (5) years of experience with software development engineering. Must have three (3) years of experience with: one or more of the following programming languages: C#, Java, C, C++, Python, SQL, or Scala; and building REST or GraphQL APIs. Must have one (1) year of experience with: working with distributed systems; database engines including at least one of the following: Microsoft SQL Server, Postgres, Aerospike, Vertica, Redis; and building micro-services and Kubernetes.
